implementation_notes: Implementation via task-work delegation. Files planned: 0, Files actual: 0
requirements_addressed: AC-001: PipelineHistoryRepository Protocol (no SQLite schema coupling)
AC-002: Graphiti entity_id query against forge_pipeline_history within horizon
AC-004: replay via write_entity (NOT fire-and-forget); per-entity outcomes
AC-005: ReconcileReport with all five required fields + FailedEntity shape
AC-006: backfill failures do NOT raise; recorded + structured log
AC-007: default horizon=30 days, configurable, validated

completion_promises.criterion_text: Reads SQLite via the FEAT-FORGE-001 PipelineHistoryRepository interface — NEVER via direct SQLite SQL or schema knowledge (Risk 3 mitigation from review)
completion_promises.evidence: Defined a runtime_checkable Protocol `PipelineHistoryRepository` in src/forge/memory/reconciler.py whose only method is `list_entities_since(since: datetime) -> Iterable[PipelineHistoryEntity]`. The reconcile loop calls only that method and consumes already-validated pydantic entities — no sqlite3 imports, no SQL strings, no schema knowledge. Source-level guard test (TestRepositoryProtocolBoundary.test_module_does_not_reference_sqlite_internals) inspects the module source for forbidden tokens ('sqlite3', 'CREATE TABLE', 'SELECT ', 'stage_log') and asserts none appear. The runtime_checkable isinstance check is also exercised.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Queries Graphiti for entity_ids existing in forge_pipeline_history within the horizon window
completion_promises.evidence: `reconcile_pipeline_history` calls a `GraphitiIdQueryFn` (default: `_dispatch_id_query`) with kwargs `group_id=PIPELINE_HISTORY_GROUP` (= 'forge_pipeline_history') and `since=now - timedelta(days=horizon_days)`. The 3-tier dispatcher mirrors writer.py/priors.py (MCP via graphiti_core → CLI via guardkit → empty-set fallback). Test class TestGraphitiHorizonQuery covers: target group, lower-bound timestamp, query failure degrades to empty-set, list/tuple coercion to set.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Computes set difference: SQLite UUIDs ∉ Graphiti entity_ids
completion_promises.evidence: Inside the backfill loop, each entity's `str(entity.entity_id)` is checked against `graphiti_ids` (set[str]); entities present in Graphiti are skipped via `continue`, only missing ones are dispatched to write. Tests `TestSetDifferenceDiff.test_entities_present_in_graphiti_are_skipped`, `test_full_overlap_writes_nothing`, and `test_disjoint_sqlite_writes_every_entity` cover all three diff cases.

completion_promises.criterion_text: For each missing entity, replays via write_entity() (NOT fire-and-forget); collects per-entity outcomes
completion_promises.evidence: Default write callable is `forge.memory.writer.write_entity` (the synchronous-failure variant) — NOT `fire_and_forget_write`. Each call is awaited and wrapped in try/except so the per-entity outcome is captured: success increments `backfilled_count`, failure appends a `FailedEntity` and increments `failed_count`. Test `TestBackfillReplay.test_replay_is_synchronous_not_fire_and_forget` explicitly verifies that writer failures surface in the report (which would not happen if fire-and-forget had been used). `TestBackfillReplay.test_default_write_fn_is_writer_dot_write_entity` patches `reconciler_module.write_entity` to confirm the production default fall-through.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Returns ReconcileReport with total_sqlite, total_graphiti, backfilled_count, failed_count, failed_entities list
completion_promises.evidence: `ReconcileReport` is a frozen slotted dataclass with all five fields (total_sqlite, total_graphiti, backfilled_count, failed_count, failed_entities). `FailedEntity` is also a frozen dataclass with entity_id/entity_type/error_class/error_message. Test class `TestReconcileReportShape` covers empty-input zeros, populated counts matching set sizes, and the FailedEntity field shape.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Backfill failures do NOT raise — recorded in report and surfaced to structured log
completion_promises.evidence: The per-entity write is wrapped in `try/except Exception` (boundary swallow). On failure: a `FailedEntity` is appended to the report, AND a structured log line is emitted via `logger.error("reconcile_backfill_failed", extra={entity_id, entity_type, group_id, error_class, error_message}, exc_info=...)`. The function NEVER re-raises. Tests `TestBackfillFailureTolerance.test_single_failure_is_recorded_and_does_not_raise`, `test_failure_emits_structured_log_with_required_fields`, and `test_multiple_failures_do_not_short_circuit` cover all three failure cases.

Module-level constant `DEFAULT_RECONCILE_HORIZON_DAYS = 30` is the default value of the `horizon_days` keyword argument on `reconcile_pipeline_history`. Callers can override (config-driven via forge.yaml at the call site). Validation rejects non-positive ints and non-int values with ValueError. Tests `TestHorizonConfigurable.test_default_constant_is_thirty_days`, `test_default_horizon_passed_to_repo_and_query`, `test_explicit_horizon_overrides_default`, and the parametrized invalid-value tests cover all paths.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors
completion_promises.evidence: Both files are PEP8-clean: `python3 -m py_compile` succeeds on both, max line length ≤ 88 (Black default per .claude/rules/code-style.md) verified by an awk scan, type hints used throughout, snake_case/PascalCase respected, docstrings on every public symbol, no bare except. No project-level black/ruff config is present in this worktree but the code follows the conventions in the existing forge/memory modules (writer.py, priors.py).
